label: Observing Sea Smoke on Lake Superior

comments.text: The sea smoke on Lake Superior was amazing this morning. It took about 30 minutes before the sun got above it, but right after it broke the top of the smoke the world turned yellow.
A wall of sea smoke off of Lake Superior this morning
Before sunrise. It is -12 degrees Fahrenheit ( -25 degrees Celsius ) That cloud sitting on Lake Superior is sea smoke
This week, I wrote about sea smoke on Lake Superior. open.substack.com/pub/bryanhan...
Sea smoke on the surface of Lake Superior

Sea smoke fills the air above Lake Superior on a beautiful winter morning on Minnesota's North Shore. Thank you for taking a look and following along! Happy Sunday! #BlueSky #Minnesota #NorthShore #landscapephotography #minnesotanorthshore
Arctic sea smoke on Lake Superior with a -30° wind chill - sweeeet!
A couple more shots from the epic fog last week off Lake Michigan.
Sea smoke rising from Lake Superior in sub zero temperatures. #Photography #LakeSuperior
A fog coming off Lake Superior as we wait for the front runners for Grandma’s Marathon. This is as good a reason as any to miss #At40
A freighter bathed in morning sea smoke on Lake Superior near Duluth. [Photo by Schauer Photo Images]

.com talking sea smoke off Lake Superior, including some new shots of that and pack ice. I was lucky enough to see this happening when driving north this past weekend, and it haunted the drive in a spectacular way. bryanhansel.substack.com/p/sea-smoke-...
Fog bank AND sea smoke, all on Gitchigami/Lake Superior at -6
Smoke on the Water- Deep Purple (it was foggy off Lake Michigan this morning)
bryanhansel.substack.com/p/sea-smoke-... Sea Smoke on Lake Superior, from .com
A wall of sea smoke over Lake Superior this morning.
Sea smoke rolling over Lake Superior this morning. #LakeSuperior

Morning fog drifts through the valley below Feldtmann Ridge, Isle Royale National Park in Lake Superior. I was there just last week, but already it feels like another lifetime.
#hiking #backpacking #nationalparks

It’s something, for sure. hurts your face in a matter of minutes. I’ve been wearing wool for a week straight. That said I’m on lake superior, and when its this cold the lake steams, as it’s so much warmer than the air. It creates sea smoke which is stunning to see. Photo credits destination Duluth
Amazing to see the Sea Smoke on Lake Superior this morning.
hard to photograph, but we're driving along the edge of lake superior and it's so warm outside and the ice is melting so quickly that the whole lake and parts of the highway are bathed in fog
Fog hanging over the Holland Marsh as we pass on the highway. Next stop Lake Superior provincial park. 🏕️
Is throwback Thursday still a thing? Sea Smoke on Lake Superior Duluth, MN
A peaceful foggy morning along the shore of Lake Superior #stunday
Sea smoke sunrises with minus 35 degree wind chill (1st pic, yesterday), Grand Marais, MN (NW shoreline of Lake Superior)
I went on an autumn weekend camping trip a few years ago along the shores of Lake Superior, and was blanketed by fog. While I missed the vistas, it was eerily comforting
Busy day just settling in...Current GOES 19 VIS RGB snapshots of the Great Lakes and close up on SONT. #onwx #onstorm ...where smoke and cloud meet.
We get 'sea smoke' on the Great Lakes when we have this cold weather as well.
Sea smoke and shore ice on Lake Superior, from 20250123.

Sea smoke on Lake Superior! Starting to post videos as they now post on Skylight, Bluesky’s short-form content platform that I’m beta testing right now! Excited to join and help create the Skylight community! #lakesuperior

Smoke on the water… Subzero temperatures lately have produced ethereal "smoke" on Lake Superior. Also called "steam fog," sea smoke "forms when water evaporates from the warm water surface and immediately condenses in the cold air above," according to the NOAA
#LakeSuperior is sending up some pretty intense sea smoke on this subzero morning!
I see you caught my favourite Great Lakes phenomenon, sea smoke/Arctic fog! Beautiful image Dave.
Purple Air says the experimental .03ppm crap is over 6000 around Lake Michigan. Not good. 😷
Arctic sea smoke appeared on Lake Michigan as frigid temperatures hit Milwaukee. The phenomenon occurs when cold air moves over warm lake water.
morning flight of around 1,500 passerines in the Keweenaw Peninsula, following strong northwest winds over Lake Superior. www.trektellen.org/count/view/5...
Really foggy today in Bayfield, WI. That’s Lake Superior out there. This is one of my favorite webcams.
Sea smoke on Lake Superior is out of control
Sea smoke because the air is -7F (windchill -24F) and somehow Lake Superior is warmer than that
"Fog in Great Lakes - Canada cut off"
That's not fog. That's smoke from Canada and California wild fires. I moved here to be by one of the Great Lakes 🤦‍♀️
Spotted a ship emitting a huge plume of black smoke out in Lake Michigan. I think it's JAMES R.BARKER. It was large and pitch black for maybe 15 minutes or so before calming down and turning white.
I just learned recently that "sea smoke" is what that's called! 😂 I usually see it more wispy on Lake Michigan, so this is beyond cool.
We get similar views from Lake Michigan and "Sea fog."
Is it weird that I was born and raised in literal Alaska and this is the first time in my life I've ever encountered the phrase "Arctic sea smoke" and it's in reference to Lake Michigan?
This was the view from my window of Lake Michigan shrouded in fog.
Recalling watching the fog come in off Lake Superior and have to admit that Canadian fog is truly superior (no pun intended even if totally apropos).
Sub-zero sea smoke this morning on Lake Superior near Duluth, MN. Present temp in Duluth at 9 pm Thursday is minus 9.
Balmy -1 degree air sweeping across the warmer surface of Lake Superior creating ghostly steam fog on this fine TH in #Duluth.
This morning sea smoke and sun over Lake Superior
The sun going down over Lake Superior with sea smoke from below zero air temps.
We all need a foggy Lake Superior morning
Yes, the personification of the fog, like NOLA heat or Lake Superior. Already added. 😁
It is not. I know the pullout you’re referencing but, this is substantially inland. All that white is fog, not Lake Superior.

SEA SMOKE...AND A WATERSPOUT? A viewer shared with me their view of the "sea smoke" that took place last Saturday on Lake Superior. A closer look shows a...possible waterspout? I'm not too familiar with cold weather waterspouts, but it does look like it. What do you think?
-17 in Tofte, MN. Insane cloud formations over Lake Superior
Cold air over the warmer water of Lake Superior creates the effect of smoke above the water.
I bet the sea smoke 💨 on Lake Superior is off the charts today!!
The sea smoke coming off Lake Superior at Two Harbours is mad looking. Cold air and warmer water causes it. See #Weather #GreatLakes

Home. It just happened at the other end of Lake Superior. We just took a drive to get my husband out of the house. There was intermittent fog as we took the back route into Wisconsin. But the Bong bridge heading back into MN was socked in. The Mighty Mac however. Nope.

I love to see the big ships on #LakeMichigan. But I don't think I've ever seen that much smoke (diesel?) coming from the stacks. Look at the gigantic, thick black smoke. This appears to have been picking up from Gary Works & heading heast. I was on the far southern shore of the Lake. #BigShips
Lake Michigan Fog, This was taken no more than 30 minutes after the photo in my last post. The weather can change quick on the great lakes! 📍 Grand Haven, Michigan, US #GrainSocial
Cloud formations over Lake Superior a few days ago
Sea Smoke on Lake Superior is beautiful today . Only -25 🌊🥶.
Sea smoke on Lake Superior. Air temp -3F, feels like -20F
The haze is on and around Lake Superior. I can say it’s almost like the fog in San Francisco…I’ve witnessed them both
Subzero temperatures create dramatic sea smoke over Lake Superior near Duluth.

This winter I learned about lake smoke. I thought I was looking at fog rising from Lake Superior. Turns out lake smoke forms when extremely cold air moves over warmer water. The lake releases heat into the air and appears to breathe. Some places never stop teaching you.
The bus stop is different when it's below zero and #LakeSuperior is covered with Sea Smoke #winterishere
